CD4⁺ Perturb-seq · Immune-checkpoint blockade · Melanoma

Perturb-seq regulators that reproduce the CD4⁺ T-cell ICB-resistance state

Using scRNAseq and Perturb-seq to investigate the novel regulatory pathways in CD4+ T cells to change melanoma ICB non-responding state.

Sade-Feldman GSE120575 · tumor CD4 Genome-scale CD4 Perturb-seq atlas Perturb2StateModel · ElasticNet + PCA Held-out r = 0.57

01The question

Immune checkpoint blockade (ICB) reactivates T cells to kill tumors and has transformed cancer treatment, but many patients don't respond. Most work on ICB response has focused on CD8⁺ killer T cells, but emerging evidence shows the CD4⁺ helper T cell is also important. Focusing on CD4⁺ helper T cells, we asked which gene-regulatory pathways push them into a non-responding state, and whether those pathways are druggable.

We compared CD4⁺ T cells from responders and non-responders in single-cell RNA-seq of 32 melanoma patients to define a non-responder signature, then integrated a genome-scale Perturb-seq atlas of ~2,600 gene knockdowns in healthy-donor CD4⁺ T cells. Because each knockdown is a direct perturbation, the model learns which regulators causally push cells toward responding or non-responding — not merely what correlates. A model trained on the resting-condition perturbation data reproduced the patient signature well, and the top knockdowns cluster into coherent pathways. The interferon pathway was the highest-ranked controller of the response state, and its downstream genes are upregulated in non-responders — so inhibiting interferon should push cells toward responding. Critically, interferon-receptor antibodies and JAK inhibitors are already-approved interferon-pathway inhibitors, turning the analysis into a concrete, testable hypothesis to sensitize non-responders.

Full figureDataMethod SignaturePredictionRegulators PathwaysDrugsLimitations

02Figure 5 — full composite

Eight panels (A–H). Click to enlarge.

03Data

CohortAccessionCompartmentPlatformCD4 cellsSamples (R / NR)
Clinical signature — scRNA-seq
Sade-Feldman melanomaGSE120575tumorSmart-seq2 TPM5,39143 (16 R / 27 NR)
Perturbation atlas — genome-scale Perturb-seq
GWCD4i Perturb-seq atlasCZI (GWCD4i.DE_stats)healthy-donor CD4⁺CRISPRi Perturb-seq7,874 profiles~2,600 KD × 3 conditions

The clinical signature comes from CD4⁺ T cells in the Sade-Feldman melanoma ICB cohort (32 patients, 43 samples). The perturbation atlas is a genome-scale CRISPRi Perturb-seq screen of ~2,600 single-gene knockdowns in healthy-donor CD4⁺ T cells, measured across three culture conditions (resting, 8-hr and 48-hr stimulation); we use the per-gene knockdown effect (z-score = log-fold-change / SE) as the perturbation matrix, subset to the 7,874 knockdown×condition profiles passing the ≥10-differential-gene filter with on-target (cis) effects masked.

04Method

Mirrors the source notebooks, step for step.

  • Signature (Panel B). CD4⁺ T cells isolated from the ICB cohort, pseudobulked per patient, DESeq2 non-responder-vs-responder → per-gene z = log₂FC / lfcSE — identical to the paper's definition.
  • Perturbation matrix. zscore layer (log_fc/lfcSE) of the atlas; cis / on-target diagonal effects masked and mean-imputed.
  • Model. Perturb2StateModel (ElasticNet + TruncatedSVD PCA, 5-fold CV, n_pcs=60, scale_y, seed 214) regressing the signature onto the perturbation matrix, per culture condition (Rest / Stim 8 hr / Stim 48 hr).

05The resistance signature recapitulates known ICB biology

Finding 1 · Panel B

The CD38⁺CD39⁺ T-eee exhaustion program (mean z = +1.7; ENTPD1/CD39, CD38, PD-1, LAG3, TIM-3) and a cytotoxic-CD4 program (mean z = +2.0; GZMB, PRF1, CX3CR1, IFNG) are up in non-responders, while responders show the LEF1/TCF7/FOXP1 stem-memory program. In the volcano, 218 genes are up in non-responders and 91 up in responders. The statistically robust resistance signal is cell-cycle / proliferation (E2F, G2-M); the responder signal is Wnt/β-catenin-TCF/LEF stem-memory.

06The Perturb-seq atlas predicts the signature strongly

Finding 2 · Panels C–D

0.57
Held-out Pearson r · Rest
0.51
Stim 8 hr
0.51
Stim 48 hr
0.29
Within-tumor replication ceiling

All three models exceed the within-tumor discovery-vs-replication upper bound (patient split, r = 0.285): the healthy-donor CD4 perturbation atlas contains regulators whose knockdown reproduces the tumor resistance state better than one melanoma half-cohort predicts the other.

07Two opposing pathways drive the axis

Finding 3 · Panels D–G

  • ResistanceKnockdown → non-responder-like: ELOF1, and the SAGA deubiquitinase pathway ATXN7L3 / USP22 / SGF29, plus LIN37 (DREAM), FUBP1, YTHDF2. Signed-KD analysis shows the SAGA pathway natively restrains the proliferation program — losing it raises the resistance genes. A third resistance term, protein UFMylation (UFL1, UFM1, UFC1, CDK5RAP3), is the strongest library-level resistance hit.
  • ResponseKnockdown → responder-like (top by most-negative coefficient): WAC, IRF9, WDR82, UBR5, IRF1, MED24, EPC1, KMT5B, DOP1A, CARMIL2. The interferon regulators rank high; native interferon signaling activates the resistance/proliferation program, so inhibiting this axis pushes cells toward the responder state.

08The responder-reproducing axis is druggable

Finding 4 · Panel H

Because a small-molecule inhibitor mimics a knockdown, the response-reproducing regulators are the ones worth inhibiting to sensitize non-responders. Screened against ChEMBL, the druggable candidates all fall on the interferon / JAK-STAT axis:

TargetRoleApproved compounds (as retrieved)# approvedNotes
IFNAR1interferon α/β receptoranifrolumab1approved antagonist antibody (SLE); blocks type-I IFN receptor
TYK2JAK-family kinasefilgotinib, upadacitinib, deuruxolitinib4approved JAK-family inhibitors annotated vs TYK2 (+ brepocitinib Ph3)
JAK2JAK-family kinase (IFN→pSTAT)ruxolitinib, momelotinib, baricitinib1212 approved inhibitors on this target

The SAGA pathway, by contrast, is both the wrong therapeutic direction (positive coefficient — inhibiting it would worsen resistance) and essentially undruggable: all 12 members return zero mechanism-of-action drugs in ChEMBL. Drug names are taken verbatim from ChEMBL get_mechanism output, never hardcoded.

09Limitations

  • Single tumor cohort. The signature rests on one melanoma dataset (43 samples); replication is a within-cohort patient split, not a second independent tumor cohort.
  • Healthy-donor atlas vs TME. The Perturb-seq atlas is from in-vitro-stimulated healthy-donor CD4 cells; it cannot contain microenvironmental signals (hypoxia, adenosine, chronic antigen).
